examples.title: 2D Transformation Using Homogeneous Coordinates
examples.description: Demonstrates translation, scaling, and rotation of a point using homogeneous matrix multiplication
examples.explanation: Each transformation is a 3×3 matrix multiplied with the 3×1 homogeneous point vector. The result maintains w=1 for affine transformations.

examples.title: Composite Transformation for Rotation About Arbitrary Point
examples.description: Rotates a point about a pivot using three transformation matrices
examples.explanation: Matrix multiplication applies right-to-left. The point is first shifted so pivot is at origin, rotated, then shifted back.

examples.title: Scaling About Arbitrary Point
examples.description: Scales an object about a fixed point using homogeneous coordinates
examples.explanation: The composite matrix scales distance from fixed point and adds back the offset, achieving scaling about any point.

examples.title: 3D Homogeneous Transformation
examples.description: Demonstrates 3D translation and rotation using 4×4 homogeneous matrices
examples.explanation: 3D transformations use 4×4 matrices. The structure extends 2D principles to z-dimension while maintaining homogeneous coordinate w.
